body: PT Pembangunan Jaya Ancol Tbk's profit plummeted 41.10% to IDR 100.59 billion in Q3 2024 from IDR 170.80 billion in Q3 2023, driven by a 2.33% decline in operating revenue to IDR 881.44 billion and a 10.89% drop in gross profit to IDR 438.34 billion. The company's revenue was mainly generated from ticket sales, hotel restaurants, and real estate, with the tourism sector contributing IDR 658.73 billion, real estate IDR 155.89 billion, and trading services IDR 169.08 billion.
title: PT Pembangunan Jaya Ancol's Profit Drops 41.10% in Q3 2024 Due to Lower Revenue and Increased Expenses.

body: PT Eastparc Hotel Tbk, a 5-star hotel management company in Yogyakarta, reported a net profit of IDR 23.32 billion in the first half of 2024, a 9.7% year-on-year decline from IDR 25.83 billion in the same period last year. The profit decline was driven by a 3.7% year-on-year drop in revenue to IDR 72.57 billion, while operating expenses surged 14% year-on-year to IDR 23.9 billion.
title: Eastparc Hotel's Net Profit Drops 9.7% to IDR 23.32 Billion in First Half 2024.

body: PT Pelayaran Nasional Bina Buana Raya Tbk (BBRM) has acquired a self-propelled barge with a capacity of 12,000 dead weight tons from PT Marcopolo Shipyard for USD 6.5 million, equivalent to 21.83% of BBRM's equity as of December 2023. The acquisition increases BBRM's assets by USD 6.5 million and liabilities by USD 4.5 million, following a total credit investment from PT Bank Mandiri Tbk.
title: PT Pelayaran Nasional Bina Buana Raya Acquires Self-Propelled Barge for USD 6.5 Million

body: Foreign investors sold a net 768.59 million shares on Wednesday, with the largest sales in digital company PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk, e-commerce company PT Bukalapak.com Tbk, and banking sector PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk. Meanwhile, they bought a significant number of shares in companies such as PT Surya Citra Media Tbk, PT Buana Lintas Lautan Tbk, and PT Aneka Tambang Tbk, among others.
title: Foreign Investors Sell 768 Million Shares in Indonesian Market, Led by Digital and E-commerce Companies

body: The Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index (JCI) strengthened to 7,528.70 at 11:24 am WIB, up from 7,512.47 at 9 am WIB, with other indices such as LQ45, IDX30, IDX80, and IDXESGL also rising by 0.69%, 0.42%, 0.83%, and 0.59%, respectively. Certain stocks, including PT Chandra Asri Pacific TBK and PT Indofood CBP Sukses Makmur TBK, rode the positive trend, while others, such as PT Barito Renewables Energy TBK and PT Unilever Indonesia TBK, experienced corrections.
title: Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index Surges Past 7,500 in Morning Session

body: Individual shareholder Iwa Sukresno Karunia increased his stake in PT Arkadia Digital Media TBK by 27.05% to over 25% by acquiring 17.40 million shares, while other investors such as Suwantara Gotama and PT Bogamulia Nagadi also made notable purchases. Conversely, PT Samuel Tumbuh Bersama and PT Goldfive Investment Capital led the largest share reductions, releasing over 12 million and 9.91 million shares, respectively, in PT Nusantara Sawit Sejahtera TBK and PT Bintang Samudera Mandiri Lines TBK.
title: Individual and institutional investors make notable changes to their stakes in various Indonesian companies.

body: PT Avia Nusantara Khatulistiwa acquired 228.81 million shares of PT Jaya Trishindo Tbk (Jati Group), gaining control of approximately 27.47% of the company's outstanding shares, while PT Startel Communication's stake dropped from 47.82% to 23.05%. Meanwhile, various investors, including PT MNC Asia Holding Tbk, DBS Bank Ltd, and individual investors, made notable purchases and sales of shares in several companies, including PT Lippo Karawaci Tbk, PT Idea Indonesia Tbk, and PT Medikaloka Hermina Tbk.
title: Multiple investors engage in significant share transactions in various Indonesian companies, including Jati Group, MNC Asia Holding, and Lippo Karawaci.

body: PT Summarecon Agung Tbk has injected IDR 485.5 billion into its subsidiary, PT Summarecon Property Development, through the issuance of 485.5 million new shares, increasing the subsidiary's paid-up capital to IDR 5.05 trillion from IDR 4.56 trillion. As of June 2024, PT Summarecon Property Development's total assets stood at IDR 14.33 trillion, while PT Summarecon Agung Tbk reported total assets of IDR 14.92 trillion, cash and cash equivalents of IDR 3.25 trillion, and total equity of IDR 13.13 trillion in the first semester of 2024.
title: PT Summarecon Agung Injects IDR 485.5 Billion into Subsidiary for Growth Promotion

body: The Indonesia Stock Exchange has lifted the suspension on PT Perdana Karya Perkasa Tbk (PKPK), an oil and gas supporting service provider, allowing trading to resume. Despite a 9.89% drop to IDR 820 per share in today's morning session, PKPK's stock has surged 121.62% over the past month, from IDR 370 per share on September 10, 2024.
title: Indonesia Stock Exchange Reopens Trading of PT Perdana Karya Perkasa After Suspension Due to Unusual Market Activity.

body: PT Lippo Cikarang Tbk plans to issue 3 billion new shares at IDR 500 per share, which will increase its authorized capital to IDR 3.5 trillion and issued paid-up capital to IDR 2.83 trillion, with proceeds to be used for business expansion. The rights issue will dilute non-participating shareholders' portions by up to 52.82%, with the company's ultimate beneficial owner, James T. Riady, maintaining an 80.83% stake.
title: PT Lippo Cikarang to Issue 3 Billion New Shares to Raise Capital for Business Expansion

body: PT Cikarang Listrindo Tbk, an independent electricity generator, is set to issue notes worth USD 500 million, a material transaction exceeding 50% of its total equity, to boost company liquidity. The proceeds may be used to pay off another bond due in 2026, with the notes to be listed on the Singapore Exchange (SGX-ST) and offered to international institutions or investors outside Indonesia.
title: PT Cikarang Listrindo to Issue USD 500 Million Notes to Boost Liquidity

body: Inflation in Indonesia is projected to decline from November 2024 to February 2025, with the price expectation index dropping to 134.3 and 155.9, respectively, due to smooth distribution of sufficient goods supplies. Meanwhile, retail sales are expected to rise in the next three months, with the sales expectation index reaching 144.4 in November 2024, before declining to 123.1 in February 2025.
title: Bank Indonesia projects inflation to decline from November 2024 to February 2025, with retail sales expected to rise in the short term before falling again.
